name: Multigrain Buttermilk Bread -Machine
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 cups Buttermilk (or 5 tablespoons dry buttermilk powder + 1 1/4 cups water)
1 1/2 cups Bread flour (plus 2 tablespoons additional flour)
3 tablespoons Red Star active dry yeast
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the bread machine pan by placing all ingredients in the pan.
2. For a 1-pound loaf, add: 1/4 cup Cracked wheat, 7/8 cups Buttermilk (or 3 tablespoons dried buttermilk powder + 1 tablespoon butter or margarine), 7/8 cup water, 1 cup Bread flour (plus 1 tablespoon additional flour), 1/4 teaspoon Baking soda, 1 cup Whole wheat flour, and 2 tablespoons Red Star active dry yeast.
- For larger loaves, use 3 teaspoons of dry yeast for all machines except Panasonic/ National machines, which require 4 1/2 teaspoons of yeast.
3. Set the machine to Light Crust setting and press Start.
4. After baking is complete, remove the bread from the pan and let it cool on a cake rack for 1 hour before slicing.
Note: For larger loaves (e.g., 1-pound), use 3 teaspoons of dry yeast for all machines except Panasonic/ National machines, which require 4 1/2 teaspoons of yeast.
